aiAct.title: EU AI Act Readiness
aiAct.lastUpdated: Build AI that's legal on the EU market — most obligations apply from 2 August 2026.

sections.heading: Does the EU AI Act apply to you?
sections.content:
Almost certainly — even if you are not based in the EU. The Act applies if any of the following is true:

• You place an AI system on the EU market, regardless of where your company is established.
• You deploy AI inside the EU.
• The output of your AI system is used in the EU, even if you operate from a third country.

If you sell into Europe, the EU AI Act reaches you — the same way GDPR did. We make that reach a non-issue.

sections.heading: What risk level is your AI?
sections.content:
The Act regulates AI by risk. We classify your use case up front:

• Unacceptable risk — prohibited outright (e.g. social scoring). Banned since 2 February 2025.
• High risk — strict obligations (risk management, documentation, human oversight, logging). Includes AI used in recruitment, hiring and worker management, credit scoring, and critical infrastructure.
• Limited risk — transparency duties. Most operational AI we build — chatbots, RAG assistants, workflow agents — sits here. The core obligation is telling people they are dealing with an AI (Article 50).
• Minimal risk — no specific obligations.

Most of what you deploy is limited-risk and straightforward. The job is knowing which of your systems cross into high-risk — and handling it before launch.

sections.heading: Transparency for interactive AI (Article 50)
sections.content:
If your AI interacts directly with people — a chatbot, a voice agent, an autonomous assistant — Article 50 requires that those people are clearly informed they are interacting with an AI, at the latest at the moment of first interaction. Burying it in your terms and conditions does not satisfy the rule.

What we implement:
• A clear, first-interaction AI disclosure on every interactive system we build.
• Labelling of AI-generated or AI-manipulated content where required.
• Accessibility-compliant notices.

This obligation applies from 2 August 2026.

sections.heading: What we deliver
sections.content:
Compliance is mostly documentation and process discipline. For every engagement we provide:

• AI risk classification — where each of your systems falls, and what that triggers.
• Transparency implementation — Article 50 disclosures built into the product.
• Technical documentation and logging — the records the Act expects from providers and deployers.
• Human-oversight design — meaningful review built into automated workflows.
• A governance pack — policies, data boundaries, and an audit trail you can show a regulator.

sections.heading: Key dates
sections.content:
• 1 August 2024 — the Act enters into force.
• 2 February 2025 — prohibited practices banned; AI-literacy duties begin.
• 2 August 2025 — obligations for general-purpose AI models.
• 2 August 2026 — most obligations apply, including Article 50 transparency and high-risk systems.
• 2 August 2027 — remaining high-risk (regulated-product) systems.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content:
Non-compliance carries significant penalties:

• Up to €35 million or 7% of worldwide annual turnover for prohibited practices.
• Up to €15 million or 3% for most other breaches, including transparency and high-risk obligations.
• Up to €7.5 million or 1% for supplying incorrect or misleading information.

For SMEs and start-ups, the lower of the fixed amount or the percentage applies.

sections.heading: Get an EU AI Act readiness assessment
sections.content: We will map your systems to their risk class, implement the transparency your products need, and hand you the documentation a regulator expects. [Request an assessment](/en#contact) or email hello@euhub-ai.com.

dataResidency.title: Data Residency & GDPR
dataResidency.lastUpdated: Your data stays in the EU — here is exactly how.

sections.heading: Where your data lives
sections.content:
For European buyers, "where does our data go?" is the first question. Our answer is concrete:

• This website runs on Google Cloud (europe-west1, Belgium).
• Client systems are deployed to the environment you choose — an EU-only region, your private cloud, or on-premise — configured per engagement by our DevOps team.
• What leaves your environment (telemetry, logs) is configured per deployment; we can keep prompts, documents and logs entirely inside your environment.
• Your data is never used to train generalised third-party foundation models without your explicit, documented consent. Where a self-hosted model is deployed, your content does not leave your environment at all.

sections.heading: Data Processing Agreement (DPA)
sections.content:
When we process personal data on your behalf, we act as your processor under a binding Data Processing Agreement compliant with GDPR Article 28. It sets out, in writing: the scope and purpose of processing, confidentiality, security measures (Article 32), our handling of data-subject requests, deletion or return of data on termination, and audit rights.

Our standard DPA is available on request.

sections.heading: Subprocessors
sections.content:
We keep a current list of subprocessors and, per GDPR Article 28(2), we do not add or replace one without giving you prior notice and the opportunity to object.

For fully self-hosted or on-premise deployments, no third-party AI or model provider acts as a subprocessor for your content.

sections.heading: International data transfers
sections.content:
If any processing involves a transfer outside the European Economic Area, we rely on the European Commission's Standard Contractual Clauses and — following the Schrems II ruling — conduct a documented transfer impact assessment and apply supplementary safeguards, such as encryption, where needed.

The simplest answer to transfer risk is not to transfer. That is why we lead with an EU-only deployment option — it removes most of this for you.

sections.heading: Security controls
sections.content:
Every deployment includes:

• Encryption in transit and at rest.
• Role-based access control and least-privilege access.
• Full audit logging.
• Single-tenant isolation in your chosen environment (on-premise or private cloud).

sections.heading: Retention, deletion and your rights
sections.content:
• Retention — we hold personal data only as long as needed for the agreed purpose.
• Deletion or return — on termination we return or delete client data within 30 days, per the DPA.
• Data-subject rights — we assist you, as controller, in responding to access, rectification, erasure and portability requests.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.content:
Data controller for this website: Engineers-incubator s.r.o., Horná 67, 974 01 Banská Bystrica, Slovakia.

[Request our DPA and data-processing pack](/en#contact) or email hello@euhub-ai.com.

sections.heading: Article 1: Introduction and Scope
sections.content:
Engineers-incubator s.r.o. ("we," "us," or "our") is dedicated to upholding the highest standards of privacy and data protection. This Privacy Policy outlines our obligations and your rights concerning the collection, processing, and protection of your personal data when you interact with our website, https://euhub-ai.com (the "Service").

This policy is drafted in strict accordance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (the General Data Protection Regulation or "GDPR") and the Slovak Act No. 18/2018 Coll. on Personal Data Protection (the "Act").

sections.heading: Article 2: Data Controller
sections.content:
Company Name: Engineers-incubator s.r.o.
Company ID (IČO): 53 741 200
Registered Address: Horná 67, 974 01 Banská Bystrica, Slovenská republika
Contact Email for Privacy Inquiries: hello@euhub-ai.com

sections.heading: Article 3: Data Collection and Purpose of Processing
sections.content:
We adhere to the principle of data minimisation and collect personal data only for specified, explicit, and legitimate purposes. We do not operate user accounts and do not collect passwords.

Information you provide directly:
• Diagnostic audit / contact requests: when you submit a form, we collect your Name, Company, Work Email, and the Problem Statement you describe.
  Purpose: to evaluate your request, respond to you, and keep a record of our correspondence (legal basis: pre-contractual steps / legitimate interest).
• Direct correspondence: your name, contact details, and message content when you email or otherwise contact us.
  Purpose: to respond to your enquiry.

Information collected automatically:
• Log and device data: IP address, browser type, device information, operating system, and referring URLs.
  Purpose: security, troubleshooting, and improving our website.
• Usage data and cookies: data from cookies and similar technologies, where you consent.
  Purpose: analytics and marketing, as detailed in our Cookie Policy.

sections.heading: Article 4: Legal Basis for Processing
sections.content:
• Contractual Necessity: Article 6(1)(b) GDPR
• Consent: Article 6(1)(a) GDPR
• Legitimate Interests: Article 6(1)(f) GDPR
• Legal Obligation: Article 6(1)(c) GDPR

sections.heading: Article 5: Data Disclosure and Transfers
sections.content:
We share personal data only with the following categories of recipients, under appropriate safeguards:

• Hosting & infrastructure: Google Cloud (Google Cloud EMEA Limited) — website hosting within the EU (europe-west1, Belgium).
• Contact-form processing: Formspree (Formspree, Inc., USA) — processes the name, company, work email and message you submit through our forms.
• Tag management & analytics: Google (Google Ireland Limited / Google LLC) — we use Google Tag Manager to load measurement and marketing tags, which may include Google Analytics, subject to your cookie consent.
• Advertising: Meta Platforms Ireland Limited — where the Meta Pixel is enabled, subject to your cookie consent.
• Legal compliance: public authorities and professional advisors, where disclosure is required by law.

International transfers: where a recipient is located outside the EEA (for example, in the USA), the transfer is protected by the European Commission's Standard Contractual Clauses and, where appropriate, supplementary safeguards.

An up-to-date list of processors is available on request at hello@euhub-ai.com.

sections.heading: 2. Data We Collect and Purpose of Processing
sections.content:
We collect personal data strictly in accordance with the principles of data minimization and purpose limitation.

Diagnostic Audit Requests: When you submit a "Request Diagnostic Audit" form, we collect your Name, Company Name, Work Email, and Problem Statement. This data is processed exclusively to evaluate your infrastructure and communicate our findings (Legal Basis: Pre-contractual steps / Legitimate Interest).

Website Analytics: We collect usage data via cookies (see [Cookie Policy](/en/cookie)) to improve our site architecture and user experience.

sections.heading: 3. AI Transparency and Automated Processing (EU AI Act Compliance)
sections.content:
In compliance with Article 50 of the EU AI Act, we provide clear transparency regarding our use of Artificial Intelligence:

Interaction with AI: When utilizing certain interactive features on our platform (such as our demonstrated "Autonomous AI Assistants"), you are interacting with an AI system rather than a human.

Automated Decision-Making: We do not subject website visitors to fully automated decision-making that produces legal or similarly significant effects (GDPR Article 22). All strategic diagnostic audits and consulting outputs are subject to human oversight and expert review.

sections.heading: 4. Data Security and "Secure Vault" Processing
sections.content: For our enterprise clients, data processed through our custom AI web-apps and agents is governed by a separate Data Processing Agreement (DPA) compliant with GDPR Article 28. We offer "Secure Vault" deployments (on-premise or private cloud) ensuring military-grade isolation and guaranteeing that client data is never used to train generalized third-party foundational models without explicit, documented consent.

sections.heading: 5. Your Data Protection Rights
sections.content: Under the GDPR and Slovak law, you possess the right to access, rectify, or erase your personal data, the right to restrict or object to processing, and the right to data portability. Where AI systems are utilized, you additionally retain the right to request human intervention, express your point of view, and contest AI-assisted outcomes. To exercise these rights, please contact hello@euhub-ai.com.

sections.heading: 1. Acceptance of Terms
sections.content: By accessing euhub-ai.com/en or engaging our services (including AI Audits, Custom AI Web-Apps, and Autonomous AI Assistants), you agree to these B2B [Terms of Service](/en/terms). Our services are strictly intended for corporate and enterprise entities (B2B).

sections.heading: 2. Provision of AI Services and Compliance
sections.content:
EUHub-AI architects, audits, and deploys custom artificial intelligence ecosystems.

EU AI Act Obligations: Where EUHub-AI acts as a "Provider" or "Deployer" of an AI system under the EU AI Act, we commit to supplying the necessary technical documentation, logging capabilities, and transparency instructions required for our clients to maintain compliance.

Data Processing: Any processing of personal data on behalf of a client during the deployment of ERP/CRM syncing or AI agents will be strictly governed by a legally binding Data Processing Agreement (DPA) executed prior to implementation, in accordance with Article 28 of the [GDPR](/en/privacy).

sections.heading: 3. Liability and Limitations of Artificial Intelligence
sections.content:
Artificial Intelligence systems, including Large Language Models (LLMs) and Agentic workflows, are probabilistic systems.

AI "Hallucinations" and Accuracy: While we engineer robust retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and limit model parameters to specific corporate knowledge bases, EUHub-AI does not guarantee that AI-generated outputs will be 100% error-free. The Client acknowledges the inherent risk of AI "hallucinations" (inaccurate outputs).

Human Oversight: The Client is solely responsible for implementing effective human oversight and verifying AI-generated outputs before relying on them for critical business, financial, or legal decisions. EUHub-AI excludes liability for indirect damages, loss of revenue, or operational failures resulting from unverified automated outputs, to the maximum extent permitted by applicable EU and Slovak law.

sections.heading: 6. Governing Law and Jurisdiction
sections.content:
These Terms and any dispute arising out of or in connection with them shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Slovak Republic.

You agree that the competent courts located in Banská Bystrica, Slovak Republic, shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any dispute which may arise out of or in connection with these Terms.

sections.heading: 7. Amendments
sections.content: We reserve the right to amend these Terms at any time. We will notify users of any changes by posting the new Terms on this Site. Your continued use of the Site after such changes constitutes your acceptance of the new Terms.

sections.heading: 8. Contact Information
sections.content:
Company: Engineers-incubator s.r.o.
Address: Horná 67, 974 01 Banská Bystrica, Slovenská republika
Email: hello@euhub-ai.com

sections.heading: 1. What are Cookies?
sections.content: Cookies are small text files stored on your device (computer, tablet, or smartphone) when you visit euhub-ai.com/en. They are utilized to ensure the proper technical functioning of our site, enhance the user experience, and provide aggregated analytical data.

sections.heading: 2. Types of Cookies We Use
sections.content:
In accordance with the ePrivacy Directive and the [GDPR](/en/privacy), we classify our cookies into the following categories:

Strictly Necessary Cookies: These are essential for navigating the website and utilizing its core features, such as maintaining your session state (e.g., sessionid). Because the website cannot function without them, they do not require user consent and cannot be disabled in our system.

Performance and Analytics Cookies: These cookies collect anonymized data regarding how visitors interact with our site (e.g., Google Analytics _ga, _gid). This allows us to measure performance, identify bottlenecks, and improve our digital architecture. These cookies are only deployed if you grant explicit consent via our cookie banner.

Marketing Cookies: Used to track visitors across different platforms to display relevant B2B advertisements (e.g., Meta Pixel _fbp). These cookies map your interactions to external advertising networks and are only deployed with your explicit, opt-in consent.

Tag management: Analytics and marketing tags on this website are loaded and managed through Google Tag Manager, a service provided by Google. Google Tag Manager itself does not set analytics or advertising cookies, but it loads the tags that do, in line with the consent categories above.

sections.heading: 3. Managing Your Cookie Preferences
sections.content: You retain full control over your data. You can manage, modify, or withdraw your cookie consent at any time by utilizing the "Cookie Preferences" settings located on our website. Additionally, you may configure your browser settings to block or alert you about these cookies, though blocking strictly necessary cookies will impact website functionality.

sections.heading: 5. Contact Us
sections.content: If you have any questions about our use of cookies, please contact us at hello@euhub-ai.com.
